description: Lockfile for the vendored SQLite native + managed stack under app/lib/sqlite/. Every file in that folder except this lockfile MUST have a corresponding entry in `files` below. At broker startup, each file's SHA-256 is recomputed and compared against `sha256`; this check is mandatory and non-bypassable. Additionally, for any file whose `expectedAuthenticodeSubject` is non-null, `Get-AuthenticodeSignature` is called and the result Status MUST be `Valid` and the SignerCertificate.Subject MUST match `expectedAuthenticodeSubject` exactly. Files with `expectedAuthenticodeSubject` null are unsigned by their upstream publisher (this is the documented integrity posture of the SQLitePCLRaw project) and rely on SHA-256 verification only. Any mismatch aborts broker startup with exit code 5. No runtime download / fetch / installer is ever attempted; updates require manual re-vendoring and regeneration of this lockfile.

procurement.method: Direct HTTP GET of canonical .nupkg files from api.nuget.org v3-flatcontainer. No package manager used.
procurement.notes: Deterministic one-time vendoring. To re-vendor: download each .nupkg from the URL recorded in each file's sourceUrl, extract, copy the listed lib/ or runtimes/ path, then regenerate this lockfile from Get-FileHash and Get-AuthenticodeSignature output.

Microsoft.Data.Sqlite.dll.expectedAuthenticodeSubject: CN=Microsoft Corporation, O=Microsoft Corporation, L=Redmond, S=Washington, C=US

SQLitePCLRaw.batteries_v2.dll.nameDrift: The upstream NuGet package is named 'SQLitePCLRaw.bundle_e_sqlite3' but the actual managed assembly identity inside the package is 'SQLitePCLRaw.batteries_v2.dll'. The on-disk filename must match the assembly identity or .NET Assembly.Load fails.
